subject: [PATCH v6 1/6] fuse: decouple fuse_ring creation from ent registration

2026-07-16.patch_summary: This patch decouples the creation of fuse_ring from entry registration in FUSE connections. Instead of creating the ring lazily on the first FUSE_IO_URING_CMD_REGISTER command, it is created during connection initialization after the server has negotiated and set FUSE_OVER_IO_URING. This change eliminates the need for open-coded handling to detect and protect against concurrent creations of the fuse_ring. The patch modifies several files in the FUSE codebase to implement this decoupling.

reviews.author: Miklos Szeredi
reviews.summary: Reviewer Miklos Szeredi questioned whether fuse_uring_register() should block if the fuse ring is not yet set up, indicating a need for further consideration.
reviews.raw_body:
What about if the fuse ring is not yet set up?  Should
fuse_uring_register() wait in that case?

Thanks,
Miklos

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author clarified that the fuse ring is created when processing FUSE_INIT reply, and userspace will get -EAGAIN if it issues a REGISTER before the ring is published.
reviews.raw_body:
The fuse ring is set up when the kernel processes the FUSE_INIT reply.
If userspace races ahead and issues a REGISTER before the FUSE_INIT
reply is processed, they get back -EAGAIN and will need to retry,
which is the same as the pre-existing behavior. The ring gets
published before fch->initialized gets set.

Not sure if this answers your question or if I'm misreading what you're asking.

Thanks,
Joanne

reviews.author: Miklos Szeredi
reviews.summary: Reviewer Miklos Szeredi questioned whether retrying the INIT reply in a loop after completing the write() operation would be good behavior, and suggested that the REGISTER op should block until initialization is complete.
reviews.raw_body:
That was my question, yes.

Retrying in a loop doesn't sound like good behavior.

I was thinking that with sync INIT the completing the write() with the
INIT reply does not mean that the connection gets initialized.

Shouldn't the REGISTER op block until initialization is complete?

Thanks,
Miklos

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges a pre-existing issue in the sync case and plans to address it in a separate patch.
reviews.raw_body:
Yes, this would be more ideal for the sync case. libfuse currently
works around this with the retry loop.

This is a pre-existing issue that's not introduced by this series
though, so I'll send a patch out for this separately.

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-07-17.patch_summary: This patch decouples the creation of fuse_ring from entry registration in FUSE connections. The ring is now created during initialization after a server has negotiated and set FUSE_OVER_IO_URING, rather than being lazily created on the first FUSE_IO_URING_CMD_REGISTER command. This change eliminates concurrent creation issues and simplifies code by removing open-coded handling in fuse_uring_create(). The patch modifies several files in the FUSE codebase to implement this decoupling.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged the issue of libfuse sending REGISTER before connection initialization, suggested a kernel fix but also offered an alternative solution to update libfuse instead.
reviews.raw_body:
We can fix up libfuse [1] to no longer send REGISTER before the
connection is initialized, so I don't think a kernel fix is strictly
needed here, but if you still prefer kernel code to coordinate this, I
can send that out.

Thanks,
Joanne

[1] https://github.com/libfuse/libfuse/pull/1561

2026-07-21.patch_summary: This patch decouples the creation of fuse_ring from entry registration in FUSE connections. The ring is now created during initialization after a server has negotiated and set FUSE_OVER_IO_URING, rather than being lazily created on the first FUSE_IO_URING_CMD_REGISTER command. This change eliminates concurrent creation issues and simplifies code by removing open-coded handling in fuse_uring_create(). The patch modifies several files in the FUSE codebase to implement this decoupling.
